title: Lesser Of Two Evils

jokes.text: Cop Department is real. The people are not actors. Most aren't even people.
jokes.setup: Parody of reality TV disclaimer about real people
jokes.punchline: Most aren't even people - subverts expectation in sci-fi context
jokes.explanation: Clever sci-fi twist on reality TV tropes with perfect economy

jokes.text: Then Why is there a smoking hole in your ceiling?
jokes.punchline: Evidence clearly contradicts his denial
jokes.explanation: Standard police procedural humor but executed competently

jokes.text: Crazy upstairs lady's been shooting doWn. You're on the top floor of this domicile.
jokes.setup: Suspect blames crazy upstairs lady for shooting down
jokes.punchline: He's on the top floor - no upstairs exists
jokes.explanation: Good logical trap joke with clear setup and payoff

jokes.text: While you're at it, unblur your face.
jokes.setup: Standard police command to put hands up
jokes.punchline: Absurd instruction to control TV censoring effect
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke about TV production breaking fourth wall

jokes.text: Bender, you said you were in this episode. No. I'm on Caught on Tape Three for what I did in the coffee pot.
jokes.setup: Expectation Bender would be on this reality show
jokes.punchline: He's on different show for unexplained coffee pot incident
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor with mysterious implication

jokes.text: Is today's lifestyle making you tense and impatient? Shut up and get to the point!
jokes.setup: Ad asking if you're tense and impatient
jokes.punchline: Immediate angry response proving the point
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic demonstration of the exact problem being advertised

jokes.text: It's like living in the year 2000.
jokes.setup: Theme park promises to relive carefree days of yore
jokes.punchline: Year 2000 is considered ancient history
jokes.explanation: Standard sci-fi joke about temporal perspective

jokes.text: Let's disco dance, Hammurabi. "Dy-no-mite!"
jokes.setup: Historical theme park mixing time periods
jokes.punchline: Ancient Babylonian king doing 70s disco
jokes.explanation: Absurd historical anachronism with pop culture references

jokes.text: If I ever wanna go back, I'll freeze myself again.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to visit historical theme park
jokes.punchline: Fry references his own accidental freezing origin story
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific humor referencing show mythology

jokes.text: You know I yearn for a simpler time of barn dances and buggy rides before life was cheapened by heartless machines. But Bender, you are a...
jokes.setup: Leela yearns for time before heartless machines
jokes.punchline: She's talking to Bender, who is a machine
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup with Fry's interruption preserving the beat

jokes.text: Give me your wallet or I'll cut you! Get a picture of me being "mugged."
jokes.punchline: It's staged entertainment for tourists
jokes.explanation: Good fake-out joke about commercialized nostalgia

jokes.text: Give me the camera too!
jokes.punchline: Actor breaks character and actually steals camera
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation turning fake crime into real crime

jokes.text: No! I'm ruined!
jokes.punchline: Actor dramatically overplays stock market crash
jokes.explanation: Simple historical recreation gag, competent but unremarkable

jokes.text: Which has yet to be deciphered. Do you know? Yes. A cop told me. It means, "Up yours."
jokes.punchline: Fry knows it's just graffiti insult
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of archaeological mystery with crude reality

jokes.text: Hey, a suicide booth! So long, suckers!
jokes.setup: Bender spots what he thinks is suicide booth
jokes.punchline: He's excited about it and ready to use it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment with dark humor and callback

jokes.text: Sorry, Bender, that's just a phone booth. What were they used for? In New York? Bathrooms.
jokes.punchline: NYC-specific cynical answer about actual usage
jokes.explanation: Good local knowledge joke about NYC reality vs intended purpose

jokes.text: "Tokens only." How does this work? I'll show you.
jokes.punchline: Physical comedy of him getting hit by turnstile
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy, functional but not innovative

jokes.text: What's this? Another bathroom? It's a mobile apartment with no rent.
jokes.punchline: Leela's cynical description of homelessness
jokes.explanation: Dark but economical observation about urban homelessness

jokes.text: The tomb of the 20th century's greatest spiritual leader... Al Sharpton.
jokes.punchline: It's Al Sharpton, not traditional religious figure
jokes.explanation: Topical humor about media figure's inflated historical importance

jokes.text: He was mummified in ceremonial vestments. We called it a "jogging suit."
jokes.punchline: It's just a tracksuit
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of ceremonial language with mundane reality

jokes.text: Move it, crap for brains! Get on, bastard man!
jokes.setup: Traffic jams as public forum for opinions
jokes.punchline: The 'opinions' are just road rage insults
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of pretentious description with crude reality

jokes.text: Here is a 20th century assembly line where cars Were constructed by primitive robots. We've come a long way, baby.
jokes.punchline: Bender (a robot) condescendingly agrees
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic moment with robot looking down on older robots

jokes.text: We've come a long way, baby.
jokes.setup: Looking at human workers described as primitive robots
jokes.punchline: Bender's condescending attitude toward humans
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's robotic superiority complex

jokes.text: My girlfriend had one! Actually, she wasn't my girlfriend... she just never closed her curtains.
jokes.setup: Fry had experience with this car model
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was peeping on neighbor
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection revealing Fry's creepy past behavior

jokes.text: Didn't I tell you to end your stories a sentence earlier?
jokes.punchline: Leela's exasperated meta-comment about his storytelling
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor about storytelling structure and TMI moments

jokes.text: I'm sorry. You work here. I should have known from that getup.
jokes.setup: Employee tells them not to touch exhibits
jokes.punchline: Fry assumes anyone dressed weird works at theme park
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Fry's assumptions

jokes.text: This is from Miller's Outpo... I mean, yeah, I work here all right.
jokes.setup: Flexo caught explaining his outfit's origin
jokes.punchline: Awkward cover-up suggesting he doesn't actually work there
jokes.explanation: Good introduction of suspicious character behavior

jokes.text: No one in New York drove. There was too much traffic.
jokes.punchline: Circular logic about NYC traffic
jokes.explanation: Classic paradoxical observation about urban transportation

jokes.text: There's a baby in there, huh?
jokes.punchline: Bender takes it literally
jokes.explanation: Simple literal interpretation joke, fits Bender's character

jokes.text: It's just like riding a bicy...
jokes.setup: Fry confident about driving using bicycle metaphor
jokes.punchline: Immediately crashes before finishing sentence
jokes.explanation: Perfect interrupted cliché with immediate physical contradiction

jokes.text: I think I got whiplash. How? You don't have a neck. I meant ass whiplash.
jokes.punchline: Anatomical impossibility corrected with crude alternative
jokes.explanation: Good robot anatomy joke with Bender's characteristic crudeness

jokes.text: I'm glad we hit something. I thought we'd never stop.
jokes.punchline: Leela's relief at crashing to stop the car
jokes.explanation: Dark practical humor about dangerous situation

jokes.text: I think I got whiplash.
jokes.punchline: Flexo repeats exact same injury claim
jokes.explanation: Simple callback establishing Flexo as Bender duplicate

jokes.text: We did all we could. You mean he's... Good as new? Yes!
jokes.setup: Dramatic medical outcome setup suggesting death
jokes.punchline: Complete recovery instead
jokes.explanation: Classic medical drama misdirection with positive outcome

jokes.text: Except for that stylish beard, he looks just like Bender!
jokes.punchline: Only difference is facial hair
jokes.explanation: Setup for evil twin trope, functional but predictable

jokes.text: No duh! We're both bending units. What's your serial number? 3370318. No way! Mine's 2716057.
jokes.punchline: Random numbers presented as meaningful coincidence
jokes.explanation: Robot bonding over technical specs, sets up mathematical joke

jokes.text: I don't get it. We're both the sum of two cubes.
jokes.setup: Fry confused by serial number significance
jokes.punchline: Mathematical property most viewers won't verify
jokes.explanation: Clever mathematical easter egg that rewards smart viewers

jokes.text: Well, I don't feel as bad as you look.
jokes.punchline: Flexo insults Fry's appearance
jokes.explanation: Standard insult joke establishing Flexo's mean personality

jokes.text: I'm just messing with you. You're okay. That's some face you've got. There's a cream for that.
jokes.punchline: Immediately follows with another insult
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - fake nice followed by cruelty

jokes.text: Your stunt did a number on my back. You mind rubbing it for me? Sure. That's it. A little lower. How's that? Lower. That's it. A little lower, though. I can't get any lower. I'll say. You're rubbing my ass!
jokes.punchline: Gradual escalation to inappropriate touching
jokes.explanation: Well-executed escalation sequence with clear uncomfortable progression

jokes.text: Check it out. Six beautiful devices. They know what you like, and they do it to within one micron.
jokes.punchline: Technical precision in sexual context
jokes.explanation: Good robot-specific humor mixing technical and sexual language

jokes.text: Spin those fans, baby. Yeah, baby! All right, mama! Spin it, baby. Gyrate it, honey!
jokes.punchline: Robots excited by mechanical parts instead of flesh
jokes.explanation: Creative robot sexuality joke with mechanical fetishization

jokes.text: I don't like this place. It's 120 with little oxygen. Shut up and hoot.
jokes.punchline: Bender dismissively tells him to participate anyway
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic with Bender's peer pressure

jokes.text: Hubba-hubba! She is built! In Mexico, I believe. And that ain't silicon. It's tungsten, and plenty of it.
jokes.punchline: Robot-specific materials and manufacturing location
jokes.explanation: Excellent robot perspective on attraction with multiple layers

jokes.text: Yeah. Look at that exhaust fan. Pervert.
jokes.punchline: Calls Bender pervert for same behavior
jokes.explanation: Good hypocrisy joke showing Flexo's judgmental nature

jokes.text: How about a lap dance for my pal here? No. That's all right. Really.
jokes.setup: Flexo offers to buy Fry lap dance
jokes.punchline: Fry's uncomfortable refusal
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward situation humor with Fry's discomfort

jokes.text: Get it? It's chlorine! It's funny because it's poisonous.
jokes.punchline: Explains why poison is supposedly funny
jokes.explanation: Dark meta-humor about explaining why dangerous things are funny

jokes.text: Keep laughing, shrimp.
jokes.setup: Fry presumably not laughing at poison joke
jokes.punchline: Flexo's threatening response with insult
jokes.explanation: Continues establishing Flexo as threatening character

jokes.text: He's bad news. I regret hitting him. Take a rage dump. He's no worse than Bender.
jokes.punchline: Leela's crude dismissal and comparison to Bender
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Leela's blunt advice

jokes.text: He is. He drinks, smokes and posts naked photos of me. That's Bender. I'm talking about Flexo.
jokes.punchline: Those are actually Bender's behaviors
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection revealing Fry's confused perspective

jokes.text: I get it. This is cute. You're jealous of Bender's new friend.
jokes.punchline: Leela diagnoses it as jealousy
jokes.explanation: Standard psychological insight joke about friendship dynamics

jokes.text: No, I'm not! Mark my words: Flexo's evil. He's the evil Bender. Rage dump.
jokes.setup: Fry insists Flexo is evil twin
jokes.punchline: Leela repeats her crude dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good callback with running gag potential

jokes.text: Everyone, get in bed with me. I have something to show you.
jokes.setup: Professor calls everyone to his bedroom
jokes.punchline: Innocent but suggestive invitation
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment with unintentionally suggestive phrasing

jokes.text: Feast your eyes on this! It's beautiful! And huge! Can I touch it? What is it, already?
jokes.setup: Reactions to mysterious object in Professor's bed
jokes.punchline: Innuendo-laden responses to innocent scientific item
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo sequence with multiple character reactions

jokes.text: It's a single atom of Jumbonium. An element so rare the nucleus alone is worth more than $50,000. How much more? 100,000.
jokes.punchline: Fry's literal question gets specific doubling answer
jokes.explanation: Good Fry moment taking figure of speech literally

jokes.text: That's why I hid it under my mattress. Can we discuss this somewhere else? Why certainly.
jokes.setup: Valuable atom hidden under Professor's mattress
jokes.punchline: Leela uncomfortable discussing it in his bed
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to awkward bedroom setting

jokes.text: When I was a little girl, I dreamed of being Miss Universe. That's pathetic. Deep down, all girls wanna be Miss Universe. Not me. Really? Maybe it's just cute girls.
jokes.setup: Amy's childhood dreams, Leela denies having same dreams
jokes.punchline: Professor's backhanded insult about Leela's looks
jokes.explanation: Good escalating insult sequence with Professor's cruelty

jokes.text: Mister Professor, right here! We should stick with people we know. Flexo's great, but... Flexo's great? That's good enough for me.
jokes.setup: Fry tries to warn against hiring Flexo
jokes.punchline: Professor takes his incomplete sentence as endorsement
jokes.explanation: Good miscommunication joke with Professor's hasty conclusion

jokes.text: You'll take eight-hour guard shifts. Bender, then Flexo, then Fry. Wait. Hold on. Let's go alphabetically. Okay. Bender, then Flexo, then Fry. Go by rank. Okay. Bender, then Flexo, then Fry.
jokes.setup: Fry tries different systems to change guard order
jokes.punchline: Every system produces same unfavorable result
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating frustration joke with perfect repetition

jokes.text: Flexo outranks me? That's "Flexo outranks me, sir!"
jokes.punchline: Flexo enforces military protocol mockingly
jokes.explanation: Good authority abuse joke with Flexo's smugness

jokes.text: Just because he has a beard you label him as evil? I've got a label for you, an ugly word called "prejudice."
jokes.punchline: Robot lecturing about discrimination
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony with robot discussing human social issues

jokes.text: I'm not prejudiced. Save it for the cross burning, Adolf!
jokes.punchline: Bender escalates with Nazi comparison
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation showing Bender's extreme rhetoric

jokes.text: Suit yourself, skinbag. I will. Good! Good! Good!
jokes.punchline: Escalating exchange of 'Good!' creating awkward loop
jokes.explanation: Good awkward standoff escalation showing mutual stubbornness

jokes.text: Eight hours of solid boredom. I'm kidding. You're a wonderful man.
jokes.punchline: Immediately backtracks with fake compliment
jokes.explanation: Continues Flexo's pattern of fake niceness

jokes.text: How did he steal the atom? By using a sleep ray. Sleep rays exist, right? No. Then I fell asleep.
jokes.setup: Question about theft method, Bender suggests sci-fi explanation
jokes.punchline: When corrected, admits simple mundane truth
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation from sci-fi explanation to simple truth

jokes.text: Keep an eye on Fry. It's possible that he did it.
jokes.setup: Bender volunteering to help search for the thief
jokes.punchline: Immediately trying to frame Fry
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Bender deflecting suspicion by throwing Fry under the bus

jokes.text: Why are you looking for Flexo in my underpants? He wasn't here 10 minutes ago, so I thought I'd check again.
jokes.setup: Bender searching Fry's underwear for Flexo
jokes.punchline: Illogical reasoning for repeated checking
jokes.explanation: Good absurd logic joke with Bender's flawed reasoning

jokes.text: And leave me high and dry in case of a scavenger hunt? No way.
jokes.setup: Fry asks to hold the map
jokes.punchline: Bender's paranoid concern about hypothetical scavenger hunt
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd paranoia showing Bender's selfish thinking

jokes.text: Wait. You're Bender. Who said I wasn't?
jokes.setup: Fry realizes he's talking to Bender not Flexo
jokes.punchline: Bender's defensive non-denial
jokes.explanation: Good reveal moment with Bender's suspicious response

jokes.text: It's a little thing called style. Look it up sometime.
jokes.punchline: Bender's condescending fashion excuse
jokes.explanation: Good Bender arrogance in obvious lie situation

jokes.text: Thank you, Bob Barker. I'm as happy as a girl can be. End statement.
jokes.punchline: Robotic 'End statement' reveals artificial nature
jokes.explanation: Good robot character humor with awkward speech patterns

jokes.text: You lost the atom, huh? You're garbage! Human garbage!
jokes.setup: Bob Barker learning about lost atom
jokes.punchline: Beloved TV host exploding with cruel insults
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of Bob Barker's wholesome image

jokes.text: $200,000? $200,001? You're closest without going over.
jokes.punchline: Price is Right rules applied inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Good game show reference with Bob Barker character consistency

jokes.text: I may be against the fur industry, but I'll skin you alive! As long as no one wears the skin.
jokes.setup: Bob Barker threatening violence despite anti-fur stance
jokes.punchline: Maintains ethical consistency even in death threats
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific joke maintaining ethical stance even while threatening murder

jokes.text: Shoot him, he's choking me! No. Shoot him, he's choking me! I don't know which one to shoot!
jokes.setup: Two identical robots fighting, both claiming to be victim
jokes.punchline: Leela can't distinguish between them
jokes.explanation: Standard twin confusion scenario with action sequence

jokes.text: Bender? You stole the atom? Yeah, but I can explain: It's very valuable.
jokes.punchline: His 'explanation' just restates the obvious motive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - thinks obvious motive is adequate explanation

jokes.text: Wait a second. Bender is the evil Bender? I am shocked! Shocked! Well, not that shocked.
jokes.setup: Revelation that Bender was the real villain
jokes.punchline: Professor's fake shock quickly becomes honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic reaction to realistic assessment

jokes.text: I'm sorry we suspected you, Flexo. But with the beard... Don't even bother! You people sicken me! I put my life on the line. This is how you repay me? You can go rot for all I care!
jokes.punchline: Flexo's over-the-top righteous indignation
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic overreaction showing Flexo's personality

jokes.text: I'm just kidding. You guys are all right.
jokes.setup: Flexo's angry rant about being wrongly suspected
jokes.punchline: Immediately drops the act and forgives everyone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flexo character moment - dramatic then casual

jokes.text: I'm so confused. The Bender I like is evil, and the Bender I hate is good. How can I live my life and not tell good from evil?
jokes.setup: Fry's moral confusion about the two robots
jokes.punchline: Existential crisis about distinguishing good and evil
jokes.explanation: Good philosophical humor about moral judgment difficulties

jokes.text: They're both fine choices. Whatever floats your boat.
jokes.setup: Fry's existential crisis about good vs evil
jokes.punchline: Leela's casual moral relativism
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response trivializing moral complexity

jokes.text: Are you people idiots? I'm still going mano a mano with this envelope.
jokes.setup: Everyone distracted while Zapp struggles with envelope
jokes.punchline: His frustrated question while still failing simple task
jokes.explanation: Good Zapp moment combining incompetence with arrogance

jokes.text: You guys are losers... but I made out with the woman from the Radiator Planet. Fry, that's a radiator. Is there a burn ward nearby?
jokes.punchline: He actually kissed an inanimate radiator and got burned
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bender moment - arrogance leading to literal painful consequence

tmdb_overview: Fry suspects Bender's new buddy, a bending unit named Flexo who looks almost exactly like Bender, of being pure evil. When a valuable atom of Jumbonium goes missing, Fry suspects Flexo, but can't tell which robot is which.
